title: Timeline changes after selecting start and end dates
excerpt: I’m trialing monday.com. I’ve set up a board and added a timeline column. Every time I add start and end dates to a timeline, it changes it to a different set of dates. I set up a completely new board in case I was doing…

posts.author: meaphotography (Maha Aldoori)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m trialing monday.com. I’ve set up a board and added a timeline column. Every time I add start and end dates to a timeline, it changes it to a different set of dates. I set up a completely new board in case I was doing something wrong. It did the same thing. How do I get the timeline to stick to the dates I add?

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @meaphotography

Welcome to the Community!

In most cases when this happens it is due to an automation set up on the board.
Can you please take a look and see if this is the case?
You can find your automations via the Automation button at the top of the board:

image1156×302 28.5 KB

If there is an automation set up, you can disable it from this screen.

If this is not the case, you may have a default value set up - this would automatically ascribe a date to the timeline column whenever you create an item.
You can check this via the icon highlighted below:

image1572×306 29.6 KB

This will load a pop up that will show you any default values set up for the board.
If there is something in the timeline field, you can delete this to prevent it from happening.

If neither of these solve the issue, I’d love to take a further look into this.
Would you be able to set up a test board and then record this behaviour in action?

You can use the online tool useloom.com to record for free and then send me a link

Best,

Dani

posts.author: thirdstring (Bob Smith)
posts.cookedHtml:
So I have a similar issue ~ not sure if the underlying issue is the same, but the effect is the same.
I have a board I populated with tasks, dates (timeline), and duration (linked to dates). Task A has a date and a duration. If I link Task B to Task A (dependency), and then add a duration, it automatically sets the date (timeline) to start on today, which in turn pushes Task A back to an earlier date. There are no default values set for dates.
The option I have is to look for my which date my dependency ends, then choose that date in the Date (timeline) field, then add the duration. But there shouldn’t be a need to do that.
It appears that you can first enter a date, then duration, then add the dependency and the date will update, so I will use that workaround for now.

posts.author: juan.polotto (juan pablo polotto)
posts.cookedHtml:
Sorry, I’m new to Monday and I’m having the same problem. Was this solved in the tool? The workaround is not feasible for me. I mean I cannot edit the whole project everytime I need to edit a date (plans are always changing)
Thanks
